{"step_by_step_thinking": "IgA pemphigus (A) is an intraepidermal blistering disease, PV (C) is a subepidermal blistering disease with blisters forming in the upper dermis, and EBD (B) is a subepidermal blistering disease with blisters forming in the lamina lucida. Bullous pemphigoid (D) is the deepest among these, as it is a subepidermal blistering disease with blisters forming in the dermis at the basement membrane zone.", "answer_choice": "D"}
